summary_text: 1) Files to add/change (smallest credible port) - Keep `note-semi-auto` as the linked-system entrypoint because it is already in the Kernel local bus: - change target: [note-semi-auto.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/note-semi-auto.sh) - Add a minimal upload helper in `scripts/local/integrations/` (Bash or Node): - recommended: add [note-draft-upload.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/note-draft-upload.sh) (or `.js`) that implements only the REST 2-step upload flow and returns JSON `{success, noteUrl, noteId}`. - In `note-semi-auto.sh`, keep current scaffold behavior and only add optional `--upload` execution branch when a draft file is available, then call the upload helper. - Update local bus metadata only if you want the bus to show the new contract: - [local-systems.json](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/config/integrations/local-systems.json) - [peripheral-adapters.json](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/config/integrations/peripheral-adapters.json) Why this is minimal: - Current repo has no existing note upload stack; [note-semi-auto.sh] is scaffold-only. - Claude’s full pipeline depends on extra scripts and runtime libs that don’t exist here, so importing everything would be a large lift. 2) Runtime / secret dependencies - Absolute minimum runtime: - shell (`bash`) and one HTTP client path (prefer `curl` for minimal dependencies) plus `node` if you keep a Node implementation. - Required secret: - `NOTE_SESSION_V5` for `_note_session_v5` cookie (same key used by Claude path). - `NOTE_XSRF_TOKEN` is optional but helpful for protected environments. - Secret storage path: - Repo already has sops secret plumbing; [import-to-keychain.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/sops/import-to-keychain.sh) already maps `NOTE_SESSION_V5`. - Runtime caveat: - Current repo has no `package.json`/node dependency install workflow, so avoid relying on `gray-matter`/npm modules. Use self-contained parsing in Bash/Node (regex/manual frontmatter parse). 3) Test strategy (minimal, safe) - Smoke: - `note-semi-auto.sh --mode smoke` verifies environment vars, paths, and that upload helper is executable. - separate smoke for upload helper: - missing `NOTE_SESSION_V5` exits with clear error - invalid markdown path error - dry-run mode if added (`--dry-run`) to validate HTML conversion + request payload shape without sending. - Integration (real API): - one manual opt-in run against a non-sensitive draft to confirm 401/403 handling and returned `noteUrl`. - Regression: - add a tiny fixture-based harness test that checks scaffold path and successful upload-branch wiring (no network) and unit-ish checks on conversion output. - do not add broad end-to-end tests until the endpoint mapping is stable. 4) Thumbnail / browser-use for first port - For the first port, keep only direct draft upload; do not bring in thumbnail or `browser-use`. - Why: - Claude’s flow adds thumbnail and browser-driven eyecatch, but this adds extra dependencies and failure modes. - Current script bus already does artifact-first workflows; best-first incremental path is upload-only. - Deferral items: - add thumbnail later if needed (`note-thumbnail-gen`) by a separate optional lane - add `browser-use` eyecatch only after upload path is stable and credentialed. - For reference, Claude’s paths are [note-thumbnail-gen.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/note-generate/scripts/note-thumbnail-gen.js) and [note-eyecatch-upload.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/note-generate/scripts/note-eyecatch-upload.sh), but they are not present/reusable as-is in this repo. 5) Concrete comparison summary - Current repo (what exists today): - only draft scaffold creation through [note-semi-auto.sh](), no network upload. - Claude-side reference: - full upload path + optional eyecatch/thumbnail via [SKILL.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/a...
